headline: Passive Indexers Are Shuffling Billions Into the Russell 2000 Just in Time for a Brutal Fed Reality Check
summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) posted a fortress quarter as passive flows shovel capital into the iShares Russell 2000 ETF (NYSEARCA:IWM). JPM printed record markets revenue while the Russell reconstitution funneled $334 billion of mechanical buying into 2,000 small-cap names. Stacking the bank’s pristine balance sheet against a basket of floating-rate borrowers shows where defensive power sits ... headline: JPMorgan (JPM) Stock Still Looks Cheap As Its 141% Run Continues
summary: JPMorgan Chase stock has returned about 141% over the past five years, yet the latest Excess Returns intrinsic value estimate suggests the shares may still trade at a discount even as broader valuation checks flag that the stock is not a clear bargain. A roughly 141% five year gain can make JPMorgan Chase look fully priced at first glance, which raises the question of how much upside is left after such a strong run.
